notes: MASTER search overhaul + focus fix (built on live v1.6.52). (1) Flexible search: a typed token now matches (case-insensitive substring) against EVERY identifier the master carries for a cabinet - the colon cab ID plus each host's pod/grid fields (gridPod, superpodId, gridGroup, grid, leafGroupId), locode, and dns/hostname. Type any identifier that exists in the data (a pod, a cab ID, a hostname) and the matching cabinets surface; no field-picking, no per-site scheme rules, no site profile needed. Cab-ID prefix hits rank first, then field hits. A single match resolves straight to the result (so the rack ELEVATION is now actually reachable); multiple matches show the existing tap-to-pick suggestion list. NOTE: search can only match identifiers present in the master - physical door-sticker labels not in the file (e.g. L1/U1) still won't resolve from the master alone; that remains future site-profile work. (2) Focus fix: the search now uses a split render - the banner + input are built once into a stable shell and only the #master-results-region refreshes on each keystroke (master_renderResults), so the input element is never torn down mid-type. This fixes the 'type one char, lose focus, must re-tap' bug - the same root cause as the long-standing v1.6.47 focus issue. Suggestion taps update the preserved input's value directly. Verification: JS parse OK (node --check, both blocks), CSS balanced 2377/2377.
